data.title: KLCI Dips At Midday As Heavyweights Weigh On Sentiment Amid Broad Regional Weakness
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia extended its softer tone at midday on April 22, with the benchmark index drifting lower as persistent selling in index heavyweights tracked weaker regional equity performance.

At 12:30pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) slipped 6.32 points, or 0.37%, to 1,709.01, as investors remained cautious following its close of 1,715.33 on the previous session.

The broader market also stayed under mild pressure, with most indices in the red, including the FBM 70, which eased 8.34 points (0.37%) to 17,842.70 and FBM EMAS declining 6.44 points (0.05%) to 12,465.69, reflecting a risk-off mood across the board.

Market breadth was negative, with losers outpacing gainers 560 to 448, while 540 counters were unchanged. A further 1,211 counters went untraded, underscoring subdued participation, alongside 35 suspended counters.

Total turnover reached 1.88 billion units, with value traded at RM1.34 billion, indicating selective trading activity despite overall weakness.

Blue-chip selling pressure remained the key drag on sentiment, with losses concentrated in financial and energy-linked counters.

Among the top losers, Malayan Banking Bhd and Public Bank Bhd peers in the financial space weighed on the index, alongside weakness in energy-related names such as Petronas Gas Bhd, which declined amid broader sector consolidation.

Trading activity remained elevated in select penny and speculative counters. Zetrix AI Bhd led volumes, while NexG Bhd and Perdana Petroleum Bhd saw active two-way flows, reflecting continued retail interest in lower-priced equities.

AirAsia X operator AirAsia X Bhd was among the more actively traded laggards, while VS Industry Bhd traded flat amid mixed sentiment in manufacturing-linked names.

Upside was led by defensive and consumer-linked counters, with Petronas Dagangan Bhd among the top gainers, alongside select plantation and consumer staples names.

Financial heavyweight insurers also saw mild gains, with Allianz Malaysia Bhd edging higher on steady defensive demand.

Overall, the midday session reflected a market lacking strong catalysts, with investors staying defensive amid global uncertainty and mixed regional cues.

Analysts expect near-term trading on Bursa Malaysia to remain range-bound, with index direction largely dictated by heavyweight banking and energy stocks in the absence of fresh domestic catalysts.

data.title: Stock Today: Bumi Armada Gains 1.35% Amid Digital Expertise-Driven Board Addition
data.description:
Bumi Armada Bhd shares traded high at RM0.375 as at 2.53 pm, up 0.005 or 1.35% from the previous close, with intraday trading ranging between RM0.370 and RM0.380 on a volume of 62,276 lots.

The counter opened at RM0.375 and moved in a tight range throughout the session, reflecting steady investor interest despite relatively muted volatility. Buying support was seen at RM0.375 with sell orders capped at RM0.380, suggesting a balanced order flow during the trading day.

The latest price movement came against a backdrop of recent corporate developments, including the appointment of Google Cloud Malaysia Country Manager Puan Raja Farhana Raja Nong Chik Najmuddin as an Independent Non-Executive Director, effective April 21, 2026.

The company said the appointment is aimed at strengthening board expertise in digital transformation and technology-driven strategy as it navigates evolving trends in the offshore energy sector.

Her appointment is seen as aligning with Bumi Armada’s broader push towards integrating digital capabilities within its operational and strategic framework.

Market sentiment towards the stock remains influenced by energy sector developments as well as broader expectations on offshore activity recovery, with investors continuing to assess the group’s positioning in a transitioning global energy landscape.

Despite the modest gain, trading activity remained relatively contained, indicating cautious accumulation as market participants weigh both sector dynamics and recent governance-related developments within the company.

data.title: Stock Today: Gamuda Gains Slightly Amid RM1.7 Billion Data Centre Win
data.description:
Gamuda Bhd’s shares exhibited firmer trading activity in the afternoon, increasing by 0.22% to RM4.46 as of 4.36 pm.

Analysts from RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research), MBSB Investment Bank Bhd (MBSB Research), and Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) have all sustained BUY recommendations for the stock, following its RM1.7 billion hyperscale data center contract win in Port Dickson. This is attributed to factors such as orderbook expansion, enhanced earnings visibility, and the strengthening of data center exposure.

The stock opened at RM4.43, moved between RM4.39 and RM4.47 during the session, and saw active trading volume of 153.59 million shares. The latest close compares with a previous indicative level of RM5.14 recorded in December 2025.

The contract from a US-headquartered multinational corporation involves the development of a single-storey hyperscale data centre covering site infrastructure works, core and shell construction and mechanical, electrical and plumbing fit-out services. Works are scheduled to begin in the second quarter of CY26 and are expected to be completed in the first quarter of CY28.

RHB Research said it maintained BUY with a revised target price of RM6.01, down from RM6.26, noting that the win reinforces Gamuda’s construction pipeline strength amid rising global demand for data centre capacity.

MBSB Research kept its BUY call with an unchanged target price of RM5.60, stating that the contract is broadly in line with expectations and keeps Gamuda on track to meet its RM50 billion to RM55 billion orderbook target by end-CY26.

HLIB also reiterated BUY, revising its target price to RM5.31 from RM5.60, while noting that despite a slower start to CY26, further replenishment from infrastructure, water and renewable energy projects alongside additional data centre jobs should support growth.

Analysts highlighted that the data centre segment now makes up about 11% of Gamuda’s RM45.5 billion orderbook, with continued tender activity across Australia, Taiwan and Malaysia expected to act as further catalysts for earnings momentum.

data.title: KLCI Snaps 4-Day Rally As Profit-Taking Hits
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia ended lower on April 22, snapping a four-day winning streak as investors locked in gains amid mixed regional market cues and softer trading momentum.

At 5pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) slipped 4.94 points, or 0.29%, to close at 1,710.39, retreating from its previous level of 1,715.33.

The broader market also eased, with FBM 70 and FBM EMAS indices edging lower at 17,835.27 and 12,618.02, respectively, after declining 15.77 points (0.09%) and 30.06 points (0.24%), reflecting a cautious tone across mid- and large-cap counters. However, the FBM SHA bucked the trend with a marginal gain of 9.06 points (0.07%) to 12,481.19.

Market participants attributed the pullback to profit-taking after recent gains, compounded by uneven performance across regional equity markets.

Turnover also moderated, with trading volume easing to 3.19 billion units worth RM2.69 billion, down from 3.45 billion units valued at RM3.18 billion in the previous session.

Activity remained concentrated in speculative and lower-priced stocks. Zetrix AI Bhd led the most active list, followed by airline operator AirAsia X Bhd and tech-related counter NexG Bhd, which saw mixed price movements amid high retail participation.

Oil and gas-related penny stock Borneo Oil Bhd remained heavily traded, while property-linked Perdana Petroleum Bhd saw selective buying interest.

Despite the pullback, market breadth remained relatively stable, suggesting underlying resilience as rotational buying persisted in select sectors.

Analysts said the market’s near-term direction is likely to remain range-bound, with investors taking cues from global risk sentiment and continued profit-taking after the recent rally.

For now, Bursa Malaysia appears to be entering a consolidation phase, with heavyweight stocks expected to continue dictating index direction in the absence of fresh domestic catalysts.

data.title: STI Dips At Open Despite Wall Street Record Rally
data.description:
Singapore equities opened slightly lower on Thursday, tracking cautious regional sentiment despite a strong overnight lead from Wall Street.

The benchmark Straits Times Index (STI) slipped 14.87 points, or 0.3%, to 4,987.85 as at 9.07am, with decliners outpacing advancers at 95 to 123. Total trading volume stood at 148.02 million shares valued at S$170.28 million.

The softer start comes after US markets climbed to fresh record highs overnight, driven by optimism following the extension of the Iran ceasefire and a solid start to the earnings season.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.69% to 49,490.03, the S&P 500 gained 1.05% to 7,137.90 while the Nasdaq Composite outperformed with a 1.64% jump to 24,657.57.

Investor sentiment has been supported by expectations of easing geopolitical tensions after US President Donald Trump extended the ceasefire with Iran indefinitely following mediation efforts by Pakistan. However, uncertainties remain as disruptions continue in the Strait of Hormuz, a key global oil supply route.

Oil-related concerns continue to linger, with prices hovering near US$100 per barrel, raising risks of inflationary pressures that could affect global growth.

Technology stocks led gains in the US, supported by strong earnings momentum, with first-quarter earnings growth tracking around 14%. Market breadth also remained positive, reflecting continued investor confidence.

Back in Singapore, derivatives trading showed mixed signals, with MSCI Singapore Index Futures at 448.40, while regional futures including Nikkei 225 Index Futures stood at 60,025.00.

data.title: Stock Today: Inari Climbs 1.68% As Bullish Breakout Drives Upside
data.description:
Inari Amertron Bhd rose 1.68% to RM1.82 as at 2.49 pm, supported by sustained buying interest following a recent technical breakout that signalled renewed upside momentum.

The stock opened at RM1.81 and traded between RM1.80 and RM1.84 during the session, hovering near its intraday high with about 15.02 million shares changing hands. Market activity remained firm with buy orders slightly outweighing sell orders, indicating continued accumulation.

The counter’s strength follows a bullish breakout above the RM1.75 resistance level highlighted by RHB Investment Bank, which said the stock could extend its gains towards RM1.90 and RM2.05 if momentum persists. The research house added that the positive setup is supported by improving volume trends and momentum indicators.

Sentiment has also been underpinned by improving medium-term prospects, with both RHB and Hong Leong Investment Bank maintaining positive calls on the stock. Analysts said while FY26 may remain soft due to weaker radio frequency demand and foreign exchange pressures, a stronger recovery is expected in FY27 driven by a new smartphone cycle and higher RF content.

They added that additional growth could come from datacom photonics, as demand for AI-driven optical connectivity and data centre applications accelerates, providing another earnings driver for the group.

Despite near-term headwinds, analysts said valuation remains attractive, supported by the group’s net cash position and improving earnings visibility, with risks tied to weaker smartphone demand and currency fluctuations.

data.title: HSIF Slips Below 26,600 Resistance, Short Bias Kept
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has maintained a negative trading outlook on Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF), advising traders to hold short positions after the index retreated sharply from key resistance levels.

The research house said HSIF fell 331 points to close at 26,161 points, after opening at 26,487 points and sliding to an intraday low of 26,085 points before the close. In after-hours trading, the index edged up slightly by 8 points to 26,169 points.

RHB Research noted that the rejection at the 26,600-point level signals a return of bearish momentum, suggesting sellers have regained control of the market. It added that the latest pullback indicates potential price weakness, supported by a declining Relative Strength Index.

The firm expects the index to trade within a range in the near term before testing immediate support at 25,100 points. A further downside could see HSIF moving towards the next support level at 24,500 points.

Traders were advised to maintain short positions initiated at 26,367 points, with a stop-loss set at 26,600 points to manage risk.

On the upside, RHB Research identified 26,600 points as immediate resistance, followed by a higher level at 27,200 points, as the market continues to navigate near-term volatility.

data.title: Market Movement Ends Mixed As Losers Edge Ahead
data.description:
The stock market movement closed on a cautious note, with decliners narrowly outpacing gainers as weakness in selected heavyweights offset pockets of late-session buying interest.

Market breadth remained negative, with 584 losers versus 565 gainers, while 596 counters were unchanged. A total of 1,014 counters were untraded and 35 suspended, reflecting a largely subdued trading environment.

Losses among key blue chips dragged on overall market performance, led by Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd, which fell sharply, alongside financial heavyweight Hong Leong Bank Bhd and conglomerate Hong Leong Financial Group Bhd.

Consumer names were also under pressure, with Heineken Malaysia Bhd and Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd retreating amid mild profit-taking.

On the upside, defensive and consumer staples counters helped cushion the broader market. Nestlé (M) Bhd led gainers, followed by Petronas Dagangan Bhd, reflecting continued demand for resilient earnings plays.

Industrial and technology-linked counters also saw selective interest, with UWC Bhd among the most actively traded gainers, while Hong Leong Industries Bhd and plantation player Batu Kawan Bhd posted modest gains.

Despite the near-balanced market breadth, the overall tone remained cautious, with investors rotating into defensives while trimming positions in cyclical and high-beta names.

Analysts said the market is likely to stay range-bound in the near term, with sentiment hinging on external cues and the performance of heavyweight stocks, which continue to dictate overall direction.

data.title: Stock Today: Genting Rises Amid Ongoing Refinancing Activities
data.description:
Genting Bhd rose 0.79% to RM2.55 as at 11.25 am, with 9.13 million shares traded, tracking steady buying interest amid a positive tone in its capital management outlook and ongoing refinancing activities in the international debt market.

The counter opened at RM2.53, moved between RM2.53 and RM2.57 before settling near its intraday high, with buying support slightly outweighing selling pressure as investors monitored developments around its US dollar-denominated perpetual bond issuance.

Sentiment around the stock was shaped by a CreditSights report stating that Genting is tapping the international debt market with proposed perpetual securities under a refinancing strategy aimed at managing upcoming obligations. The group is marketing two tranches of subordinated perpetual bonds with initial price thoughts of 7.95% and 8.625%, with proceeds expected to be used largely to refinance its US$1.5 billion bond due in January 2027.

CreditSights noted that final pricing could tighten from initial guidance, reflecting investor demand and relative value versus regional peers, while also advising participation in both the new issuance and related tender offer.

The refinancing move comes as Genting navigates elevated capital expenditure and higher leverage linked to expansion initiatives and stake-related investments, although the group continues to be supported by diversified earnings across gaming, plantations, power and oil and gas.

Analysts also highlighted that about 82% of Genting’s revenue is derived from gaming operations across Singapore, Malaysia, the United Kingdom and the United States, providing a stable core cash flow base despite ongoing expansion pressures.

data.title: Stock Picks: Sime Darby Property And QES Group
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has identified Sime Darby Property Bhd and QES Group Bhd as stocks with near-term upside potential, citing bullish technical breakouts supported by strong momentum.

The research house said Sime Darby Property is poised to extend its gains after breaking above the RM1.35 resistance level with strong trading volume. It noted that the stock formed a bullish Marubozu candlestick, signalling firm buying interest and positive momentum.

RHB Research expects the counter to test the next resistance levels at RM1.43 and RM1.51 if the upward trend continues. However, it cautioned that a drop below the RM1.27 support level could weaken sentiment and trigger a reversal.

Meanwhile, QES Group has also entered a bullish phase after closing above the RM0.42 resistance level. The stock registered a “higher high” candlestick pattern, indicating strengthening upward momentum.

The research house said QES is likely to target the next resistance levels at RM0.45 and RM0.48, supported by sustained buying interest. On the downside, it flagged RM0.39 as a key support level, with a breach potentially leading to renewed selling pressure.

RHB Research noted that both counters are currently riding on positive technical setups, with momentum-driven trading expected to guide near-term price movements.

data.title: Wall Street Hits Records As Ceasefire Hopes Lift Sentiment
data.description:
US stocks climbed to fresh highs on Wednesday, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq closing at record levels, as investors responded positively to an extension of the Iran ceasefire alongside a strong start to the earnings season.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 340.65 points or 0.69% to 49,490.03, while the S&P 500 gained 73.89 points or 1.05% to 7,137.90. The Nasdaq Composite outperformed, advancing 397.60 points or 1.64% to end at 24,657.57.

The rally came after US President Donald Trump extended the ceasefire with Iran indefinitely following mediation efforts by Pakistan, although tensions remain unresolved as the US naval blockade of Iranian ports continues and disruptions persist in the Strait of Hormuz.

The waterway, which accounts for about 20% of global oil supply, remains a key uncertainty for markets. Iran’s parliament speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf said a full ceasefire would only be meaningful if the blockade is lifted.

Markets have been buoyed in recent weeks by expectations of a possible de-escalation, even as risks tied to energy supply and inflation linger with oil prices hovering near US$100 per barrel.

“Everyone’s kind of sick of it… clearly, the market is looking for a beneficial outcome or some kind of decent outcome here,” said Stephen Massocca, senior vice president at Wedbush Securities in San Francisco.

“Earnings have been good – now, will they continue to be good if we continue to be at war – it’s going to lose a little bit of its oomph. That said, in my world, there’s still tremendous value, there’s a lot of really cheap stuff out there.”

Technology stocks led gains, with the S&P 500 tech index rising 2.31%, supported by chipmakers. Micron Technology surged 8.48% to a record close, while the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index extended its rally to a sixteenth straight session.

Corporate earnings continued to underpin sentiment, with first quarter growth tracking around 14%, according to LSEG data. GE Vernova jumped 13.75% after raising its annual revenue forecast, while Boston Scientific gained 8.99% on strong quarterly results. Boeing climbed 5.53% after posting a smaller-than-expected loss.

However, not all stocks advanced. United Airlines fell 5.58% after guiding lower profit expectations due to rising fuel costs.

Market breadth remained positive, with advancing stocks outnumbering decliners on both the New York Stock Exchange and Nasdaq, as investors continued to balance optimism over earnings with lingering geopolitical risks.

data.title: Ringgit Opens Lower Amid Ongoing US-Iran Talks
data.description:
The ringgit opened lower against the US dollar on Thursday, weighed by cautious sentiment as traders awaited clearer outcomes from ongoing United States-Iran negotiations, according to Bernama.

At 8 am, the local currency eased to 3.9550/9600 versus the greenback, from 3.9510/9550 at Wednesday’s close.

Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the dollar-ringgit pair has been hovering around RM3.95, as shifting optimism and pessimism kept trade directionless in the near term.

He added that West Texas Intermediate (WTI) and Brent crude prices were rising to US$92.96 and US$101.69 per barrel, respectively, amid a lack of progress on the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, which continued to weigh on sentiment and the ringgit.

“At the same time, benchmark US equities closed higher overnight, with the S&P 500 hitting another record high, while US Treasury yields rose, signalling that prospects of higher inflation may prompt the Federal Reserve to maintain a restrictive monetary policy stance,” he told Bernama.

At the open, the ringgit traded mostly higher against a basket of major currencies.

It strengthened against the British pound to 5.3389/3456 from 5.3414/3468 at Wednesday’s close and rose versus the euro to 4.6293/6352 from 4.6408/6455, but edged lower against the Japanese yen to 2.4798/4831 from 2.4794/4821.

The local currency traded mixed against its ASEAN peers.

It rose versus the Singapore dollar to 3.1000/1042 from 3.1030/1063 at Wednesday’s close and appreciated against the Thai baht to 12.2567/2795 from 12.2717/2902, but weakened against the Indonesian rupiah to 230.2/230.5 from 229.9/230.2 and was flat against the Philippine peso at 6.57/6.59 from 6.57/6.58 previously.

data.title: Malaysian Equities Open In Green, Tracking US Record Highs
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened higher on Thursday, with all major indices advancing in early trade as sentiment tracked Wall Street’s record-setting performance and improved global risk appetite.

As at 9.16 am, the FBM KLCI rose 7.47 points or 0.44% to 1,717.86, moving within an intraday range of 1,714.41 to 1,718.38.

Broader market indices also opened in positive territory. The FBM70 index gained 60.03 points or 0.34% to 17,895.30, while the FBM Emas rose 49.12 points or 0.39% to 12,667.14.

The FBM Emas Shariah index advanced 27.79 points or 0.22% to 12,508.98, while the FTSE4Good Bursa Malaysia index climbed 3.92 points or 0.38% to 1,022.89, reflecting firmer ESG-linked sentiment at the open.

Market tone was supported by advancing regional sentiment following a strong Wall Street session, where US equities closed at fresh records on easing geopolitical concerns and optimism over corporate earnings.

Technology-led gains in the US, alongside stronger-than-expected earnings growth of around 14% for the S&P 500 in the first quarter, helped reinforce global risk appetite.

Despite the positive start, investors continue to monitor external risks, including ongoing geopolitical developments in the Middle East and oil price volatility, which have kept crude near US$100 per barrel.

Within the local market, trading activity was led by active counters including AMS Advanced Material Bhd, which remained among the most heavily traded stocks following its recent listing, while broader sector movement stayed largely positive.

data.title: Asian Stocks Track Wall Street To Record Highs Led By Japan
data.description:
Asian shares tracked Wall Street higher on Thursday (Apr 23), led by record highs in Japan, South Korea and Taiwan, as investors shrugged off higher oil prices from more shipping woes in the Gulf and focused on strong corporate earnings.

Overnight, the S&P 500 climbed 1 per cent and the Nasdaq jumped 1.6 per cent to close at new record highs, helped by a strong start to an earnings season that has eased concerns about the health of the US consumer despite rising energy prices from the Iran war.

That was despite oil prices gaining for a fourth straight day. Iran on Wednesday captured two container ships seeking to exit the Gulf via the Strait of Hormuz, tightening its grip on the crucial waterway, as a fragile ceasefire hangs in the balance for now

Brent crude futures rose 0.5 per cent to US$102.45 a barrel, having jumped 3.5 per cent overnight to cross back above US$100.

MSCI’s broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan rallied 1 per cent to a record high as tech heavyweights surged in the region. 

Markets in Japan, South Korea and Taiwan vaulted to records for a second day, with the Nikkei topping the 60,000 mark.

China’s blue chips rose 0.3 per cent and Hong Kong’s Hang Seng index slipped 0.3 per cent.

“Markets have been remarkably effective at looking through risks – and may continue to be. But the list of risks is growing as resolutions remain elusive,” said Laura Cooper, global investment strategist at asset manager Nuveen.

“The dissonance cannot hold indefinitely … At some point, the weight of what is being ignored could become the only one that matters.”Wall Street futures slipped in Asia after the earnings-driven rally, with the Nasdaq futures off 0.2 per cent and S&P 500 futures down 0.3 per cent.

Shares of GE Vernova surged 13.75 per cent after the power equipment maker raised its annual revenue forecast on the AI boom, and Boeing advanced over 5 per cent after a smaller-than-expected quarterly loss.

Electric automaker Tesla reported a surprise positive free cash flow in the first quarter, but its projection of sharply higher spending plans on AI and robotics drew scepticism from investors, with its shares last down 2 per cent after the bell.

Treasuries were also mostly steady despite the jump in oil prices.

The two-year U.S. Treasury yield held at 3.8064 per cent, after edging up 1 basis point (bp) on Wednesday. 

The 10-year yield inched 1 bp higher at 4.3094 per cent, after finishing little changed overnight.

Currencies were mostly calm, with the dollar holding onto small gains from overnight. The euro was steady at US$1.1709, just above a 10-day low of US$1.1691, having lost 0.3 per cent overnight.

“It is questionable whether financial markets are correctly pricing the reality that supply constraints will remain an issue for some time,” said Skye Masters, head of markets research at the National Australia Bank.

Reuters

data.title: Japan’s Nikkei Crosses 60,000 Level For The First Time
data.description:
Japan’s Nikkei share average crossed the 60,000 level for the first time on Thursday (Apr 23), as SoftBank Group and other technology stocks gained and risk sentiment improved after United States President Donald Trump extended the ceasefire with Iran.

The Nikkei was last up 0.34 per cent at 59,790, as of 12.10pm (8.10pm, Singapore time). The broader Topix was down 0.7 per cent at 3,718.59.

Chip-related shares rose, with Advantest and Tokyo Electron up 2.65 per cent and 0.81 per cent, respectively.

Technology investor SoftBank Group jumped 8.9 per cent.

Fibre optic cable maker Fujikura gained 1 per cent.

Trump said the indefinite extension of the ceasefire followed a request by Pakistani mediators. However, the US Navy’s blockade of Iranian ports remained in effect, and Iran seized two ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

Of all the Nikkei components, 42 stocks rose and 158 fell, with five trading flat.

Reuters

data.title: Bursa Extends Gains At Midday As FBM KLCI Rises 0.49%
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia extended its positive momentum at midday, with the FBM KLCI rising 0.49% to 1,718.70 as broader market sentiment stayed firm across most indices.

The FBM 70 outperformed, climbing 0.79% to 17,976.15, while the FBM Small Cap index added 0.32% to 12,521.74. The FBM Emas rose 0.51% to 12,682.84 and the FBM Emas Shariah gained 0.52% to 1,024.25, reflecting broad-based buying interest across mid and large-cap counters.

Trading activity remained heavy with turnover concentrated in several high-volume stocks, led by AMS which saw 870.71 million shares traded as it held at 29 sen after an earlier decline. ZETRIX eased 0.5 sen to 85.5 sen while AirAsia X fell 7 sen to RM1.23, making it among the most actively traded counters during the session.

On the gainers’ board, MPI led advances, rising RM0.98 to RM36.62, followed by Kawan Renergy which added RM0.30 to RM5.99. HLFG also gained RM0.16 to RM19.30, contributing to the positive tone among selected financial and industrial names.

On the decliners’ list, Nestle slipped RM0.44 to RM98.30 while F&N eased RM0.36 to RM30.56. UTDPLT dropped RM0.32 to RM33.40 and MISC declined RM0.22 to RM8.08, reflecting mild profit taking in defensive and plantation-linked stocks.

data.title: China’s Yuan Weakens As Dollar Regains Strength Amid Ceasefire
data.description:
The central parity rate of the Chinese currency renminbi, or the yuan, weakened 15 pips to 6.865 against the U.S. dollar Thursday, according to the China Foreign Exchange Trade System.

In China’s spot foreign exchange market, the yuan is allowed to rise or fall by 2 percent from the central parity rate each trading day.

The central parity rate of the yuan against the U.S. dollar is based on a weighted average of prices offered by market makers before the opening of the interbank market each business day.

US President Donald Trump has announced an indefinite ceasefire in the Middle East, easing tensions in the region, which also saw oil prices retreating.

data.title: Bitcoin Powers Ahead While Ethereum Tracks Gains In Crypto Rally
data.description:
Bitcoin surged into April 22 with strong momentum, holding above the US$76,000 level after a fresh leg-up driven by institutional buying and improving macro sentiment, while Ethereum followed with more modest gains as investors rotated back into risk assets.

The world’s largest cryptocurrency climbed roughly 1%-2% over the past 24 hours to trade near US$76,800, extending a broader rebound from February lows and pushing toward recent highs just below US$78,000.

Ethereum, the second-largest token, meanwhile, lagged slightly but remained firmly in positive territory, rising about 0.7%-1.4% in tandem with Bitcoin’s advance.

The rally was underpinned by a powerful mix of institutional accumulation and macro tailwinds. A major US-listed firm, formerly known as MicroStrategy, unveiled a US$2.5 billion Bitcoin purchase, its largest since 2024, reinforcing corporate demand and boosting market confidence.

At the same time, easing geopolitical tensions and expectations of diplomatic progress in the Middle East lifted broader risk appetite, spilling over into crypto markets alongside gains in US equity futures.

The upswing has also spilled into crypto-linked equities, with firms tied to digital assets, particularly those with direct Bitcoin exposure, outperforming as leverage to the rally amplifies gains.

With Bitcoin flirting with multi-month highs and Ethereum stabilising, analysts are watching for a decisive breakout above the US$78,000 level for Bitcoin, which could open the path toward US$80,000 and beyond.

For now, the latest rally marks a continuation of crypto’s renewed bullish cycle, driven less by speculation and more by institutional capital, macro alignment and strengthening technicals, a combination that could define the next leg of the market.

data.title: Asian Stocks Slip As Ceasefire Doubts Weigh On Sentiment And Oil Supply Outlook
data.description:
Emerging Asian equities slipped on Wednesday as investors weighed uncertainty over the Middle East ceasefire and the broader economic impact of disrupted crude flows, with markets reacting to concerns over inflation and growth pressures linked to ongoing energy supply risks.

The MSCI gauge of emerging Asia equities fell 0.4% from recent highs, with regional benchmarks across Southeast Asia including Malaysia, Singapore, Indonesia and the Philippines each easing about half a percentage point. The pullback came as markets assessed the implications of prolonged disruption to oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz, a key global energy shipping route that remains effectively closed amid geopolitical tensions.

Sentiment was also influenced by US President Donald Trump’s announcement of an indefinite extension of the Iran ceasefire, although uncertainty remains over whether all parties will adhere to the arrangement. Analysts noted that the lack of clarity has left markets pricing in a prolonged standoff rather than a stable resolution, keeping risk premiums elevated.

MUFG analysts said the situation points to continued disruption to energy flows through the Strait of Hormuz, suggesting that markets are adjusting to a structural shift in supply conditions rather than a temporary shock. The outlook has reinforced concerns among energy-importing economies in Asia, where inflationary pressure remains a key focus.

Currency markets reflected the cautious tone, with the Indonesian rupiah weakening to 17,185 against the US dollar, close to its record low. The Malaysian ringgit also slipped 0.3% to 3.9520 while the Philippine peso eased 0.3% to 60.12.

Economists noted that Bank Indonesia is expected to maintain its policy rate at its latest meeting, prioritising financial stability amid external volatility. DBS Bank said potential fuel price adjustments and their inflation impact will be key factors in shaping future monetary policy direction.

In contrast, Taiwan’s equity market bucked regional weakness, rising more than 1% to reach a record high, supported by strength in technology shares despite broader risk-off sentiment across Asia.

Reuters

data.title: Foreign Exchange Rates April 22, 2026
data.description:
The closing foreign exchange rates, sourced from Bank Negara Malaysia and recorded at 5pm on April 22, 2026, provide a comprehensive overview of currency fluctuations for the day.

These rates, crucial for investors and businesses alike, reflect the relative strength or weakness of major global currencies against the Malaysian Ringgit. They serve as a vital indicator for assessing international trade competitiveness, investment opportunities and overall economic trends in the global market.

The exchange rates are as shown below:
